2606.20231.title: Thermodynamic Measure of Intelligence
2606.20231.paper_summary: Can intelligence be measured? We propose that intelligence can be defined as the lawful amplification of rare but valid futures: a system increases the probability of outcomes that would be unlikely under passive dynamics but remain admissible under the constraints of the domain. We start with the premise that an intelligent system must model the world and its own place within it. Because the system is part of the world it models, this leads naturally to recursive self-simulation: the system represents futures in which its own actions are part of the trajectory. Our central results give a necessity statement and a conditional near-sufficiency statement connecting this architecture to a precise thermodynamic measure of lawful amplification of rare-valid futures: high rare-valid lift is impossible unless the internal simulation identifies rare-valid futures with high fidelity; conversely, when rare-valid fidelity is high and the simulation contains an effective policy, the achievable lift approaches the actuation-limited optimum. Thus recursive self-simulation is not merely a plausible feature of intelligence but, under the stated assumptions, is necessary and nearly sufficient for high thermodynamic intelligence. The resulting framework makes intelligence measurable on a universal scale, from passive matter and feedback controllers, large language models, and humans as text generators to Maxwell-demon-like information engines.
2606.20231.paper_summary_zh: 智慧能否被測量？我們提出智慧可以被定義為合法放大稀有但有效的未來：一個系統增加在被動動力學下不太可能出現的結果的概率，但在領域的限制下仍然是可接受的。我們從一個前提出發，即智能系統必須對世界及其在其中的位置進行建模。因為該系統是它所建模的世界的一部分，這自然導致了遞歸自我模擬：該系統代表未來，其中它自己的行動是軌跡的一部分。我們的核心結果給出了一個必要性陳述和一個條件近充分性陳述，將這一架構與合法放大稀有有效未來的精確熱力學測量聯繫起來：除非內部模擬以高保真度識別稀有有效的未來，否則高稀有有效的提升是不可能的；相反，當稀有有效的保真度高且模擬包含有效策略時，可達到的提升接近於激活限制的最佳值。因此，遞歸自我模擬不僅僅是智慧的一個合理特徵，而是在所述假設下，對於高熱力學智慧是必要的和幾乎充分的。由此產生的框架使智慧在普遍尺度上可測量，從被動物質和反饋控制器、大型語言模型到作為文本生成器的人類以及類麥克斯韋妖的信息引擎。
2606.20231.author: Ishanu Chattopadhyay et.al.
